examples.title: Algorithm: Color Thresholding
examples.description: Applies color thresholding to an image
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ColorThresholding(image, minHue, maxHue, minSat, maxSat, minVal, maxVal)
INPUT: image (RGB image), minHue, maxHue, minSat, maxSat, minVal, maxVal (threshold values)
OUTPUT: thresholded image

BEGIN
  FOR EACH pixel IN image
    CONVERT pixel TO HSV
    IF HSV(pixel) WITHIN RANGE [minHue, maxHue] AND [minSat, maxSat] AND [minVal, maxVal]
      SET pixel TO WHITE
    ELSE
      SET pixel TO BLACK
  END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm applies color thresholding to an image by converting each pixel to HSV and checking if it falls within the specified range.

examples.title: Python: Color Thresholding
examples.description: Applies color thresholding to an image using OpenCV
examples.code:
import cv2
import numpy as np

# Load the image
image = cv2.imread('image.jpg')

# Convert the image to HSV
hsv_image = cv2.cvtColor(image, cv2.COLOR_BGR2HSV)

# Define the threshold values
min_hue = 0
max_hue = 180
min_sat = 0
max_sat = 255
min_val = 0
max_val = 255

# Apply the threshold
thresholded_image = cv2.inRange(hsv_image, (min_hue, min_sat, min_val), (max_hue, max_sat, max_val))

# Display the thresholded image
cv2.imshow('Thresholded Image', thresholded_image)
cv2.waitKey(0)
cv2.destroyAllWindows()
examples.explanation: This code applies color thresholding to an image using OpenCV by converting the image to HSV and using the `inRange` function to apply the threshold.
